arts.ttl: Preston aim to banish the humdrum after long wait for Wembley glory
arts.sum: Championship club face elite opposition in Aston Villa but the Lancashire town is gripped by Cup fever ‘I’ve had to tell my mum not to bother with Mother’s Day,” quips Preston fan Tom Bates. The 29-year-old has forgone the purchase of flowers, cards and the prospect of a Sunday roast to buy a ticket at Deepdale for North End’s first FA Cup quarter-final in 59 years. The past six decades have brought Preston six relegations and promotions, a third and fourth division title and a solitary Lancashire Senior Cup. Since they triumphed in the League One playoff final in 2015 their seasons have been humdrum: they have finished between seventh and 14th each season and this is the first time they have got beyond the fourth round in either cup. It has given supporters such as Bates, who saw his first match when he was two, little to celebrate. The visit of the Champions League side Aston Villa to Lancashire will be one of the biggest days in the club’s recent history, played out in front of a vociferous crowd of 23,400. A tie at Wembley awaits the winners. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Chelsea transfer news: Aston Villa 'leading the race' to sign attacker in summer window
arts.sum: Aston Villa are reportedly regarded as the frontrunners to secure a deal for a Chelsea attacker during the summer transfer window.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa leading the race as £33m-rated Chelsea star made available
arts.sum: Aston Villa are reportedly leading the chase to sign a promising attacking player who looks set to be made available by Chelsea in the upcoming summer transfer window. The Blues could apparently be open to letting Noni Madueke go at the end of this season, and Villa look to be in a strong position to […] The post Aston Villa leading the race as £33m-rated Chelsea star made available appeared first on CaughtOffside.

arts.ttl: Preston without six players for Aston Villa clash as Paul Heckingbottom confirms 'problem'
arts.sum: Preston without six players for Aston Villa clash as Paul Heckingbottom confirms 'problem' Preston North End host Aston Villa in the FA Cup quarter-finals this weekend

arts.ttl: Six players who have signed for Aston Villa but haven't played for the club yet
arts.sum: It was a tricky window for the club, as they worked around the clock before the June 30 accounting deadline to raise funds while also recruiting the likes of Lewis Dobbin, Ian Maatsen, Enzo Barrenechea, and Samuel Iling-Junior. After struggling for game time in Italy, Iling-Junior joined Middlesbrough on loan for the remainder of the season in January.

arts.ttl: Unai Emery lifts lid on Donyell Malen conversation after difficult Champions League decision
arts.sum: Malen joined Villa from Borussia Dortmund in a deal worth around £20m in January but was left out of Emery’s Champions League squad for the knockout stages following the loan arrivals of Marcus Rashford, Marco Asensio, and Axel Disasi. Unai Emery put an arm around Donyell Malen before he left for international duty with the Netherlands after the forward's difficult start at Aston Villa .

arts.ttl: Report: Aston Villa now leading Tottenham in race to sign one of Chelsea's 'best players' this summer
arts.sum: Both Aston Villa and Tottenham Hotspur have been busy in the last few transfer windows, although the results have been different so far. The Villans...

arts.ttl: Preston’s Milutin Osmajic in contention for FA Cup quarter-final clash with Aston Villa amid racism charge
arts.sum: Milutin Osmajic could face Aston Villa amid allegations of racism against Burnley’s Hannibal Mejbri

arts.ttl: From Aston Villa & Preston to Everest base camp
arts.sum: As his old clubs Preston and Aston Villa prepare to meet in the quarter-finals of the FA Cup, Simon Grayson tells BBC Sport about managing in Nepal.

arts.ttl: Preston's Osmajic in contention for Villa clash amid racism charge
arts.sum: Preston forward Milutin Osmajic is in contention to face Aston Villa in the FA Cup quarter-finals, days after being charged by the FA for allegedly making racist remarks towards Burnley's Hannibal Mejbri last month.
